title: Noticing Present Perfect in real text
methodology_notes: Tier-1 awareness item; should precede any controlled drilling. 4 instances per ~70 words is intentional density (Schmidt's noticing hypothesis).

exercises.rubric: Read the message. Underline every Present Perfect verb form. Then answer the question below.
exercises.context: An informal email written just after arriving home from a trip.

items.text: Hi Mark, I've just got home from the airport. I haven't unpacked yet, but I wanted to write before I forgot. The trip has been amazing — we've visited three cities and I've taken about a thousand photos. I'll send you some when I'm less tired!
items.follow_up: Why does the writer use Present Perfect rather than Past Simple here? Look for clues in the time references.
